fresh_facts.claim: US marines seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship attempting to evade the American blockade on Iranian ports, with President Trump stating forces blew a hole in its engine room.
Trump stated the US blockade on Iranian ports 'will remain in full force' until a deal on Iran's nuclear program and regional activities is reached.
Trump threatened to 'knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge' or 'blow up the whole country' if no deal is reached before the two-week ceasefire expires around April 22.
IRGC naval forces halted and forced two oil tankers (Botswana- and Angola-flagged) to turn back in the Strait of Hormuz under armed pressure, including firing warning shots.
Iran's joint military command condemned the US ship seizure as 'armed robbery' and 'piracy,' vowing retaliation and to re-enforce restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz.
Reports indicate Iranian retaliation via drones against a US ship following a Gulf of Oman seizure incident.
Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi called US actions 'clear signs' of disingenuousness ahead of peace talks.
Iran rejected second-round peace talks in Islamabad, citing US 'excessive demands,' though the US delegation including JD Vance still headed there despite Iran's refusal.
Pakistan intensified diplomacy over the past 24 hours to hold talks Tuesday amid ceasefire expiry risks, with Iran's participation uncertain.
Trump claimed key issues in Iran negotiations have been resolved and he is considering a $20 billion asset unfreeze for enriched uranium.

A Provisional Leadership Council comprising President Masoud Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Alireza Arafi holds interim power following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death.
The Assembly of Experts building in Qom was struck on March 3, complicating succession processes.
Prediction markets price the odds of Iranian regime collapse at 1% by April 30 and 3.2% by May 31.
The World Bank political stability index for Iran stands at -1.58 for 2024, reflecting ongoing decline.
The Reza Pahlavi succession market rose to 4.5% YES for June 30, up 0.5 points in the last 24 hours.
Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf criticized hardliners as 'extremists' undermining a US deal, facing pressure alongside Foreign Minister Araghchi.
The Iranian government retracted compensation promises for US-Israeli strike damage, sparking public anger.
Trump claimed US gas prices will fall following the conclusion of what he termed an 'Iran war.'
Trump called Iran-US talks 'highly unlikely' to extend the current ceasefire agreement.
Trump accused Iran of 'total violation' of the ceasefire with the Strait of Hormuz closure.
Iran indicated it may pause Hormuz shipments as a de-escalation measure in ongoing negotiations.
The US shifted its negotiating position from demanding a permanent nuclear ban to proposing a 20-year limit on Iranian nuclear activities.
French President Macron urged de-escalation between the US and Iran.
US official Mike Waltz noted Iran's 'desperation' for a deal following the Hormuz reversal, stating there is no 'trust' approach in negotiations.
The ceasefire currently in place masks unresolved nuclear issues, Lebanon destabilization concerns, and rising terrorism risks.
No new reports of active protest movements or civil unrest in Iran were reported in the past 24 hours, though the January crackdown reached 100 days.
No visible fractures such as IRGC defections have been reported despite crackdown pressure and economic deterioration in Iran.
The Trump administration is expanding its naval crackdown globally against Iran-linked vessels carrying oil or arms, according to Wall Street Journal reporting.
Oil prices rose on disruption fears following US ship seizures and IRGC tanker interceptions in the Gulf.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran confronts an acute multi-front crisis as the April 22 ceasefire deadline approaches: US naval blockade enforcement (ship seizures, global vessel crackdown), Iranian Strait of Hormuz counter-actions (tanker interceptions, threatened closures), escalating US military threats, collapsed diplomatic momentum despite marginal negotiating shifts, and internal regime fractures with succession council governance following Khamenei's death.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US marines seized Iranian-flagged cargo ship with Trump threatening to 'blow up the whole country' and destroy all Iranian infrastructure if nuclear and regional demands are not met before ceasefire expiry around April 22, while Iran retaliated with drone strikes and forcibly turned back foreign oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.
